hypothesis._variant_note: v2 family member 1 of 5 (H1: frame-only stretch). Same 3-leg GARP recipe as the rejected baseline (hyp_growth_garp_3leg_20260428); only TopK and rebalance cadence change. Tests whether the IS turnover failure was a frame artifact rather than a signal-mix problem.
hypothesis.thesis_statement: The 3-leg GARP composite (grow_opprofit_qoq, grow_roe_yoy, val_bp_industry_rel) on broad mainboard universe with TopK=120 + 20-day rebalance delivers cost-adjusted excess return vs CSI500 with max_drawdown<=32% and annual_turnover<=3.5x — i.e., the prior 11.83x turnover was a TopK=50/rebalance=10d frame artifact, not a signal-mix problem.
hypothesis.mechanism: Doubling rebalance cadence halves the count of rebalance events per year. Tripling TopK widens the cutoff margin so single-name reorderings inside the basket are absorbed without forcing a swap. Both effects directly reduce turnover; signal-side mechanism (operating-profit growth + ROE growth + industry-relative B/P) is unchanged from the rejected baseline that already measured rank_icir=0.466.

source.title: Growth-strategy v2 variant family — H1 frame-only

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: Tripling TopK from 50 to 120 widens the swap-distance margin around the cutoff but ALSO makes more names eligible to swap. Net turnover effect is empirically uncertain and may settle in the 4-7x range, still above the 3.5x floor. If so, H1 alone does not solve the turnover problem and H2/H4/H5 (composite or universe changes) become required.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: The rank_icir=0.466 measured with TopK=50 is assumed to survive at TopK=120. Diluting the basket by 2.4x typically reduces extreme-tail spread; rank_icir could shrink toward 0.3-0.4. Still well above the 0.030 floor, but worth measuring vs the H4-tightened-universe baseline to attribute IC change to TopK vs filter.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: IS gate rejection on (annual_turnover > 3.5x) — that falsifies the frame-only hypothesis. IS gate rejection on (max_drawdown > 0.32) means TopK=120 alone does not provide enough diversification ballast and a defensive component is required.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: At 4-7x predicted turnover and 10 bps slippage + stamp tax, expected annual cost drag is ~80-160 bps. cost_adjusted_sharpe=0.724 in the prior 11.83x run corresponds to ~240 bps drag; halving turnover should restore ~100 bps of drag, lifting cost_adjusted_sharpe toward ~0.85-0.95 IF rank_icir holds.

prescription._note: v2 H1 frame-only stretch: TopK 50->120, rebalance 10->20, max_pos 0.025->0.012 (feasibility: 0.012 * 120 = 1.44 >= 1.0). Components and universe unchanged from the rejected baseline.
